RQ1: Abnormal Cholesterol Prevalence by Sex and Weight Status

Zhan_Zhao

2026-04-28

Task Description

Initialize

Define Data Directories

getwd()
## [1] "/Users/zhaozhan/nhanes-abnormal-cholesterol-young-adults"
#Set file directories workflow 

project.dir<-"/Users/zhaozhan/nhanes-abnormal-cholesterol-young-adults"
data.dir<-file.path(project.dir,"01 Data")
output.dir<-file.path(project.dir,"02 Output")

# check dir if exiting
# dir.exists(data.dir)
# dir.exists(output.dir)

#Set data directories workflow 
# Dependent variables
hdl.chol.file<-file.path(
  data.dir,
  "HDL_L.xpt"
)

t.chol.file<-file.path(
  data.dir,
  "TCHOL_L.xpt"
)

demo.file<-file.path(
  data.dir,
  "DEMO_L.xpt")

bmi.file<-file.path(
  data.dir,
  "BMX_L.xpt")

Load and Clean Data

#load hdl.chol
hdl.chol<-read_xpt(hdl.chol.file)|>
  clean_names()

#load t.chol
t.chol<-read_xpt(t.chol.file)|>
  clean_names()

#clean data
hdl.chol.c<- hdl.chol|>
  filter(!is.na(lbdhdd))|>
  select(
    seqn,lbdhdd
  )

t.chol.c<- t.chol|>
  filter(!is.na(lbxtc))|>
  select(
    seqn,lbxtc
  )

#merge data
Abnormal_cholesterol<-left_join(t.chol.c,
          hdl.chol.c,
          by="seqn")

# fullfill dataset :Non-HDL-C = Total Cholesterol（TCHOL_L）− HDL-C（HDL_L）
Abnormal_cholesterol<-Abnormal_cholesterol|>
  mutate(
    non.hdl.c = lbxtc - lbdhdd
  )|>
  rename(high.total.chol = "lbxtc",
         low.hdl = "lbdhdd",
         high.non.hdl = "non.hdl.c")



#BMI Sex ...
demo<-read_xpt(demo.file)|>
  clean_names()

bmi<-read_xpt(bmi.file)|>
  clean_names()

demo.c<-demo|>
  select(seqn,
         riagendr,
         ridageyr,
         ridreth1,
         ridreth3,
         sdmvpsu,
         wtmec2yr,
         sdmvstra)|>
  filter(ridageyr>=18 &ridageyr<=26)

bmi.c<-bmi|>
  filter(!is.na(bmxbmi))|>
  select(seqn,bmxbmi)

#merge the Primary Dataset
nhanes.young<-left_join(demo.c,
                        Abnormal_cholesterol,
                        by="seqn")


nhanes.young<-left_join(nhanes.young,
                        bmi.c,
                        by="seqn")

nhanes.young<-nhanes.young|>
  filter(!is.na(high.total.chol)|
           !is.na(low.hdl)|
           !is.na(high.non.hdl))

Create Analytic Variables and Survey Design

nhanes.young <- nhanes.young |>
  mutate(
    flag.high.tchol = high.total.chol >= 200,
    flag.low.hdl = low.hdl < 40,
    flag.high.nonhdl = high.non.hdl >= 145,
    flag.any = flag.high.tchol | flag.low.hdl | flag.high.nonhdl,

    sex = case_when(
      riagendr == 1 ~ "Male",
      riagendr == 2 ~ "Female",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    bmi.cat = case_when(
      bmxbmi < 25.0 ~ "Underweight or normal weight",
      bmxbmi >= 25.0 & bmxbmi < 30.0 ~ "Overweight",
      bmxbmi >= 30.0 ~ "Obesity",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# keep the dependent variabels in specific file path
processed.dir <- file.path(data.dir, "processed")
if (!dir.exists(processed.dir)) {
  dir.create(processed.dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
saveRDS(
  nhanes.young,
  file = file.path(processed.dir, "nhanes_young_cholesterol.rds")
)


nhanes.design <- nhanes.young |>
  as_survey_design(
    ids = sdmvpsu,
    strata = sdmvstra,
    weights = wtmec2yr,
    nest = TRUE
  )

pre.overall <- nhanes.design |>
  summarise(
    any_abnormal = survey_mean(flag.any, na.rm = TRUE),
    high_tchol = survey_mean(flag.high.tchol, na.rm = TRUE),
    low_hdl = survey_mean(flag.low.hdl, na.rm = TRUE),
    high_nonhdl = survey_mean(flag.high.nonhdl, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

print(pre.overall)
## # A tibble: 1 × 8
##   any_abnormal any_abnormal_se high_tchol high_tchol_se low_hdl low_hdl_se
##          <dbl>           <dbl>      <dbl>         <dbl>   <dbl>      <dbl>
## 1        0.298          0.0168      0.184        0.0183   0.122     0.0153
## # ℹ 2 more variables: high_nonhdl <dbl>, high_nonhdl_se <dbl>

Figure 1. Overall Prevalence of Abnormal Cholesterol Measures

#figure 1: Overall prevalence of abnormal cholesterol measures
fig1.data <- pre.overall |>
  select(
    any_abnormal,
    any_abnormal_se,
    low_hdl,
    low_hdl_se,
    high_nonhdl,
    high_nonhdl_se,
    high_tchol,
    high_tchol_se
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "measure",
    values_to = "value"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    type = ifelse(grepl("_se$", measure), "se", "estimate"),
    measure = gsub("_se$", "", measure)
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = type,
    values_from = value
  ) |>
  mutate(
    category = case_when(
      measure == "any_abnormal" ~ "Any abnormal cholesterol",
      measure == "low_hdl" ~ "Low HDL cholesterol",
      measure == "high_nonhdl" ~ "High non-HDL cholesterol",
      measure == "high_tchol" ~ "High total cholesterol"
    ),
    percent = estimate * 100,
    lower = (estimate - 1.96 * se) * 100,
    upper = (estimate + 1.96 * se) * 100,
    category = factor(
      category,
      levels = c(
        "Any abnormal cholesterol",
        "Low HDL cholesterol",
        "High non-HDL cholesterol",
        "High total cholesterol"
      )
    )
  )
print(fig1.data)
## # A tibble: 4 × 7
##   measure      estimate     se category                 percent lower upper
##   <chr>           <dbl>  <dbl> <fct>                      <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 any_abnormal    0.298 0.0168 Any abnormal cholesterol    29.8 26.5   33.1
## 2 low_hdl         0.122 0.0153 Low HDL cholesterol         12.2  9.24  15.2
## 3 high_nonhdl     0.181 0.0134 High non-HDL cholesterol    18.1 15.4   20.7
## 4 high_tchol      0.184 0.0183 High total cholesterol      18.4 14.8   21.9
fig1 <- ggplot(fig1.data, aes( x = category, y = percent)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue", width = 0.65) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(percent, 1)),
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 4
      ) +
  scale_y_continuous (
    limits = c(0, max(fig1.data$upper, na.rm = TRUE) + 8),
      breaks = seq(0, 60, by = 10)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Prevalence of Abnormal Cholesterol Measures",
      x = NULL,
      y = "Percent"
    ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text (size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size =10),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 11)
      )

fig1

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(output.dir, "figure1_abnormal_cholesterol_measures.png"),
  plot = fig1,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

Figure 2. Prevalence by Sex and Weight Status

#tabele combine sex and bmi
# Recode sex and BMI categories

prev.sex <- nhanes.design |>
  filter(!is.na(sex)) |>
  group_by(sex) |>
  summarise(
    prevalence = survey_mean(flag.any,na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  mutate(
    group = "Sex",
    category = sex
  ) |>
  select(group, category, prevalence, prevalence_se)

prev.bmi <- nhanes.design |>
  filter(!is.na(bmi.cat)) |>
  group_by(bmi.cat) |>
  summarise(
    prevalence = survey_mean(flag.any, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  mutate(
    group = "Weight status",
    category = bmi.cat
  ) |>
  select(group, category, prevalence, prevalence_se)

fig2.data<- bind_rows(prev.sex, prev.bmi) |>
  mutate(
    percent = prevalence * 100,
    lower = (prevalence - 1.96 * prevalence_se) * 100,
    upper = (prevalence + 1.96 * prevalence_se) * 100
  )
print(fig2.data)
## # A tibble: 5 × 7
##   group         category            prevalence prevalence_se percent lower upper
##   <chr>         <chr>                    <dbl>         <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 Sex           Female                   0.229        0.0157    22.9  19.9  26.0
## 2 Sex           Male                     0.367        0.0348    36.7  29.9  43.5
## 3 Weight status Obesity                  0.503        0.0425    50.3  42.0  58.6
## 4 Weight status Overweight               0.283        0.0734    28.3  13.9  42.7
## 5 Weight status Underweight or nor…      0.199        0.0335    19.9  13.3  26.5
fig2.data <- fig2.data |>
  mutate(
    category = factor(
      category,
      levels = c(
        "Female",
        "Male",
        "Underweight or normal weight",
        "Overweight",
        "Obesity"
      )
    )
  )

fig2 <- ggplot(fig2.data, aes(x = percent, y = category, fill = group))+
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes( xmin = lower, xmax = upper),
    height = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes( label = round (percent, 1)),
    hjust = -0.25,
    size = 4
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, max(fig2.data$upper, na.rm = TRUE) + 8),
    breaks = seq(0, 80, by = 10)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Prevalence of Abnormal Cholesterol by Sex and Weight Status",
    x = "Percent",
    y = NULL
  ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 11)
    )
## Warning: `geom_errorbarh()` was deprecated in ggplot2 4.0.0.
## ℹ Please use the `orientation` argument of `geom_errorbar()` instead.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
fig2
## `height` was translated to `width`.

ggsave(
    filename = file.path(output.dir, "figure2_abnormal_cholesterol_by_sex_bmi.png"),
    plot = fig2,
    width = 9,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )
## `height` was translated to `width`.
